Um, bigger party. Yeah, I have it. I was going to give it to everybody, but I decided not to. No, you can get one from Terry. Do we have that in the package? Okay, anybody that wants one, we can get one for them. Yeah, there should always be a PS in the letter. And in, if you can't think of anything else to do with the PS. What you can do with it. Is simply restate the main benefit in the headline and don't forget, like I said in the beginning, if you act now. You too can be 37 pounds slimmer in the next 60 days. Okay, that letter has credibility. How do you write it? You sit down unless you're a stock market expert. You sit down with somebody and get this information. We sure do. We interview the person exhaustively. We have researchers work for us and research the, the mag the financial articles, etc, etc. Yeah, we're very, very careful about that. Just say I want to ask Terry something. Do you have any of the list? Would you bring them up? Bring up two or three of them. I'll ask questions while she's doing it. Yes. I think you've checked on some of this already. You clarified the set amount of profits. To what? The close of the sale. They are all in those the sheets. They're probably they are all there and it's going to be more probable for you to read those and write them out. Yeah, even with the high percentage of phone orders with credit cards, do you still always use a business reply on the local leader? No, no, I don't. I think maybe I should, but I don't. Sometimes, you know, a lot of times I don't even let people reply by email anymore. I know that if you're going for inquiries, you know, if there's been a test that Claude Hopkins did 50 years ago and I reconfirmed it. And if you're generating leads and you give the people three options, option A is you can only call option B is you can only write option C is you can call or write the first option only call will generate three times as many leads as either of the other two. Isn't that curious? Okay. Yes. Two step mailing when you send a little letter. Send the what? Step one of a two step mail when you send the first letter and it says the next letter was just like this. How much of a lead time is there in between? I wouldn't leave very much. Three days. Three days. Yeah. Almost nobody mails their customers often enough. The biggest mailers in the country mail their customers at least once and sometimes twice a week. I think you should mail your customers at least once a month. You should mail something to them. I'm going to. I'm going to conclude except for those of you who want to stay later and have questions. By showing you. Visually and graphically and tactfully. I want to make everything come together. Exactly. What I recommend that people do. I told you that I have been working with Sam fish by. And that he is a real pro. He's just a delight to work with. Sam has invented a new advertising technique. Which is the most profitable newspaper format that he has ever used. Which means it's probably the most profitable one that's ever come across. And this is when you have a long copy ad. That you would have to normally put on two pages and remember especially when you're selling something expensive the more you tell the more you sell. Well there's a number of ways to do two page ads. One of the ways that I've done them is you just put one on the left and one on the right. Okay. And you put it in the middle. Of the paper. That's that's a good idea except it's very difficult to get that position because generally that's taken. Or it's by someone who advertises all the time or editorial. Another way to do a two page ad. Is you and I use this one a lot is you do it on an odd numbered page like page three. And then it says continued on next page and it's page four. Sam came up with something that's really, really clever. He calls it the pull out the pull through sheet. And see this ad right here. This ad ran last Tuesday in the Rocky Mountain News and it's a two page ad. And it happened to run on page 18. So when you get to page 18, it says free video tapes to make extra and you don't know what it is. There's a little blurb as much as $10,000 a day. And if you want to know what that ad says. What you have to do, you'll find is you have to pull it out. And then that's the entire ad. Now, there's a couple of beautiful things about this. Not everybody reads every page of any publication. So when you do this, that means that you're they are more likely to put the first page up front. But if someone doesn't catch that and they're going through the publication, they get another shot in this case, you've got another shot to get them on page 52. So we got them on page 18 and on page 52. And it's the same thing in reverse. If you just get this happening, you say, oh, that's kind of interesting. And then you have to pull it out. Another minor advantage is you can get more copy in because you can use the gutter, which is not normally used. Now, this particular ad is it says free video tapes reveal 11 new ways to make extra money in 1996. And this is selling a subscription to my newsletter. And I think it might be worthwhile for me to read to you the closing copy of this. And what you should know about this ad is many of you will be familiar with much of the words of the copy. There's a lot of validation. There's a lot of testimonials. There's a lot of I really and so on and so forth. But I want you to listen to the way that I am closing this ad and asking for a new subscriber. You've got to remember now I am writing to strangers. These are people who have never heard of me. It says, OK, that's it. You've just read a detailed description of what you can learn by watching the video tapes of Halbert's recent seminar and reading the exact same money making reports you gave everybody who attended. Why is Halbert willing to send you these reports and video tapes and send them free? The answer is simple. It's a bribe. It's a shameless bribe to get you to sign up for a trial subscription to his unique marketing and money making newsletter called the Gary Halbert letter. Here's the deal. If you will agree to a one year trial subscription to his newsletter, Halbert will send you all of the video tapes and the money making reports you've just read about and he'll send you all this free. And to make it even better, if you decide to cancel your subscription at any time within the year, he will immediately send you a full and complete pro rata refund and subhead. He will still let you keep the free video tapes and reports. Notice that I made it a year, not first 10 days or something. However, once you start reading Halbert's newsletter, you'll probably want to keep getting it forever. You see when it comes to marketing and making money, the Gary Halbert letter probably reveals more valid inside secrets than any other publication on earth. If you have any doubts whatsoever about this, just read the comments from actual subscribers, including the founder of entrepreneur magazine, which are reproduced in the box below. Look, you need this kind of hard nose info. It is not a luxury. If you want to make some fast serious money, this kind of information is crucial. And please don't forget, if you subscribe to the Gary Halbert letter right now, you will receive immediately. You will immediately receive via first class priority mail all of the amazing video tapes and reports described in this page. How much is a subscription to the Gary Halbert letter? Because dot, dot, dot, it is very unusual. Anyway, also in close with this letter is a sealed white envelope that contains info that may or may not be of interest to you. What happened was I gave an incredible seminar at the Century Plaza Hotel in Los Angeles and people all over the world are raving about it. Many people are begging me to do another seminar like that, but I won't. I'll never give another seminar like that one because it just took too much out of me. It was draining. Yes, I did make a lot of money from that seminar. I charged each person $6,250 and it was sold out, but I don't care. There's no amount of money that could make me risk my health by working that hard again. However, I have figured away a few people who are interested could get the tapes of that seminar as a free gift. Actually, I'm never going to straight out sell those tapes because even though it might sound conceited, subhead, I consider them priceless. Would you like to have the tapes of that enormously valuable seminar? Look, if you're just a dreamer or a lazy wannabe, those tapes will do you no good whatsoever. On the other hand, if you are serious about making serious money, well then, those tapes can be your own personal passport to riches. But you know what I hope you do with it? I hope you frame it. Why? Therefore, I'll have my secretary call you in a few days to make sure you've received everything I've sent to you. Peace. Now that's the way you handle a customer. Now, they get the letter, the dollar bill, and now they have the brown envelope. And this contains just what it was promised to contain, which is seven back issues of my newsletter. The February 2nd, 1989 issue with me and Joe Sugarman on a boat and how I explained to them all the different price points and how price is relative. The April 8th, April 6th, 1989 issue, which really tells the books that you need to read to learn to write copy, how to go about writing copy, why you shouldn't pay attention to idiots like Herschel, Gordon Lewis, and contains a lot of the information that I've taught you right here in this seminar. Then they get the March 26, 1991 issue, and this one was written especially for you. The sub-adhere is multi-level marketing sucks. And this tells people why they should not get into multi-level marketing. I remember talking about trying to get Howard Ruff not to go into multi-level marketing, which took him down the tubes. And after I talk about this, I conclude the newsletter with a P.S. P.S. do not misrepresent what I wrote about Howard Ruff. He's a very intelligent, very savvy, moral man. I suspect his IQ is far higher than mine, and I've learned a lot from him. I read his letter, listen to his lectures, and I have enormous respect for him. It's just that when he's on MLM, he, like everybody else, loses his ability to think straight. Okay? The next one is December 30, 1991, and it tells the things that I believe to be true that you should take into consideration if you want to publish a newsletter. And I give the rules for it. You might be interested in those rules. Rule number one, do not publish a newsletter unless you're a real expert in what you're talking about, because you're going to, people read your newsletter, they are going to know just exactly the degree of your expertise. And if you're an idiot, what you're going to do is widespread it. And people do that. I put always use your name in the title of your newsletter. Make your newsletter look like a personal letter. Have it contain nothing but words. People don't want those little computer graphics. Always mail your newsletter first class mail. Your newsletter should never contain paid advertising. Never lie to your subscribers. Make all legitimate refunds on a prompt basis. Somebody tries to screw you, sue their asses off. And a big important one to me is never stop, never start believing your own PR. You know, if I believe all of the bad things that people say about me, I could never get out from under the covers in the morning. If I believe all the good things that people say about me, I'd walk out in the Atlantic Ocean and try and walk on it. None of it is true. I am pretty much like all of the rest of you. I have a lot of good things about me, a lot of bad things and a lot of mediocre things. I think I write a really good newsletter. Another piece of advice I give people is don't make me into a savior. One of the things that people imbue strong personalities with is qualities that they do not in fact possess. You know, you can play good rock and roll music, so you'd make a great present. Ain't so. What I can do is write a good newsletter. That's about the end of it. And the last one I wrote in there is like a message to me that says always be true to yourself. The next newsletter is January 29, 1990. Well, I'm sorry. The next newsletter is my 100th issue that contains the floppy disk. It's March 31, 1995, and you'll all remember that one. It's the 100th issue with the floppy disk. And the last one that they get is January 29, 1996, which starts Dear Friend and Subscriber. What is the best way in the world, the best real, no bullshit way to make a lot of money if you are starting with nothing? And I want to ask you something. After they have read my ad or letter to subscribe to my newsletter, and after they get the Federal Express package, and they've read my welcome letter with the dollar bill attached to it, and after they've read these seven back issues, they didn't know that they were getting, what will they think? What will have happened to them? They'll be knocked on their ass. They'll be knocked on their ass. What will they think about me? I'm for real. And you know what else they'll think? Some of them will just despise me. You arrogant, conceited son of a bitch, a small percentage will. But the people who have anything going for them whatsoever, and most of them will, or they wouldn't have answered the ad, what I'm doing here is what I suggest you do is you knock them dead. You surprise them. You don't try to wheeze them out and give them the tiniest little bit that you can give away with. Wham it into them. Then they open the white envelope. And this says, here's how you can get the tapes of my $6,250 seminar of the century, absolutely free. And the way you become that is becoming is by becoming a lifetime subscriber, which costs $2,855. Now, now let's do the math. We're guessing now we spent $500 to get 20 people who subscribe to make forth to take in $4,000. Let's say I'm going to FedEx all these 20 people that package. How many of those 20 people do you think will become a lifetime subscriber? Right now. Actually, it's the people who will do it immediately is about two. That's immediately the people who will do it eventually is about six. So that's 2855 times two. That's 5710 plus the 4,000. That's for starters. And that's before I con these people into coming to a room like this and paying me more money to hear me in person and to make all the rest of my offers, et cetera, et cetera. What you have just seen is a formula for real that I am doing myself. I practice what I preach. This is the way you should do it. Now, just in case you're also interested in it. At the same time, we send the letter or right after they get all the free reports, which are in maximum money and they get the two videotapes. And although it's not in here, there will also be a letter in here offering them the opportunity to buy something. Now, some of you are saying that you do not have a product and I can tell you there has been a transition several transitions in my life in the last year and a half. I'm not going to tell you about this. I'm not going to tell you that I'm not going to tell you that I'm not going to tell you. There has been a transition, several transitions in my life in the last year and a half. I'm not going to tell you about all my sad stories because mine are nothing compared to some of the other people's sad stories. But, you know, I had some blows that I had to overcome and I lost my way for a while. But there's something that has occurred to me, you know, I don't know very many people who have made more money than I have and have lost more money than I have. I've done it more often than I have. And it finally occurred to me something very liberating is I really don't much care about money for the same reasons that other people do. I honestly do not. I think you've made an awful lot of money. Some of you others have made an awful lot of money. You know, if you've made $15 million, making $2 million more does not change your life materially. I have already done all the real reasons I made money. You know, the real reason I made money was to show my dad to make him speechless and I did it because he said I could never do it. And the real reason was to go back to my high school reunion and walk in and say, you know, have all the girls, you know, just flock over to me, you know, and when I could look at them and say, where were you when I was 16 and perpetually horny and you wouldn't look at me, you know, now you want to talk to me, right. But, you know, I've had the Rolls Royces and the cars and the boats and all of that stuff out of there. And basically, that stuff's kind of nice. But, you know, living in a one bedroom apartment with two cats and being a healthy, relatively attractive bachelor living in South Beach ain't too bad. And now I've decided, hey, you know what, I don't want to do anymore. I don't want to do anything that I don't want to do. I really don't. I don't want to sell diet pills anymore and I don't want to sell facelifts in the jar. What I want to sell is what I offer the most value. That's what we call it, facelift in the jar. You know, someone said, what are we going to do when Ernie dies? And I said, oh, I've got that covered. We're going to make sure that he looks really great and people look at him and say, well, he looks really great, even though he's dead. He said, yeah, because he uses facelift in a coffin. Okay. If any of you are interested, I got a little bit of a deal for you. This kind of an interesting deal. Now I want to tell you something. The deal isn't as good for you as it is for me. And I make no apologies for that because this is my life. This is my life's work. I'm nearly 60 years old. This is everything I put into. But if you don't know what to do and one thing, if you've never done this, you need to experience what it's like to pick up the telephone and have somebody on the other end of the phone, giving you their credit card number. You need to experience what it's like going to the mailbox and opening a letter, opening a novel for pulling the checkup. I'm telling you, it changes your life. So here's the deal. I am doing this myself. I'm not waiting to do it. We are doing it as we speak. This is doing it. If you want to do it, we have mailing lists here. You can just take the mailing list home with you. We'll give you envelopes and you go get the envelopes printed. We'll give you the sales letter. And the only thing you will have to change in the sales letter unless you want us to take the orders. But I don't even care about the control on the money is where it's like, in fact, this is 1-800-000. You got to get yourself an 800 number, which takes about a day, right? Just call up, get yourself an 800 number. And if you can't do this, something real, real wrong. So, you know, I mean, it's all there and you're selling the worthwhile product. Now, here's, you can lose on any of this. You can lose on anything. But here's, let's say that you mailed a thousand of these letters. And I want to tell you, I will not continue to do it. You don't have to pay me anything to do this. There's like no franchise fee, no nothing fee. This is of all the things I've tried to think of. I've tried to think of something where there couldn't be a loser, you know? Now, there can, but I want you to, I want you to get in mind what happens if you mail these letters and don't get any orders? What do I get out of it? Squat, right? So, I have a vested interest in making it work. So, if you take all this stuff home and do it, and for a thousand letters, you're going to spend 500 bucks. We don't know if it is true or not, but we are using for this illustration, you will get say 20 people who call and subscribe to the newsletter. And that's 4,000 bucks, right? Well, actually it's not because I'm going to take 27 of that. So, instead of getting 200, you will get 197 minus 27, which is what, 170? That times 20, you're going to get half of the profit. So, it's 0, 0, 14. That's $5,400. All right, be picky. $3,400. Subtract the 500. And what's that? 2,900. And if it works that way, you'll get half of that. What about refunds? Well, you don't get any of my back end, you don't have to take any of the refunds. Take it from there. That's it. I mean, you can assess the risk for yourself. Now, why am I doing this? What's that like? You want part of my lifetime subscription? No. No, I don't blame you for that. No, that's it. That's all the deal there is. There's no back end for you. Really, there is not. This is a better deal for me than it is for you. But again, I've got almost 60 years in doing this. And it's a really good deal for those of you who are going to sell mouth freshener or something else. This is here, written by the best copywriter in the world, selling the best product in the world with no fee to get into. I don't want to do too much of a pitch for it because I'll tell you the truth. I only thought of this. Honestly, I didn't have this seminar to do this. I just thought of this. Why don't I do this? But let me tell you why I'm doing it. What about the mailing list? Oh, no, by the way, you can't do that because it's illegal. I mean, you do not buy a mailing list. You rent it. That means you're only allowed to use it for one mailing. And you have to tell the broker what that mailing is. You have to give them a sample of it. No, you cannot do that. Okay, why am I doing this? The reason I'm doing it is because for the rest of my life, I don't want to do anything that I don't want to do. And what I want to do more than anything is write my newsletter. You've seen the show of hands again. How many of your lives have been changed by reading my letter just somewhat? Just look at that. That's a great thing. And there's thousands of others that have it. That's what I do best. And I'm on fire to do it. It's my passion. I haven't written a letter to get a new subscriber for six years. You know what I do? I write letters for dull turkeys like Shabak. And they get hundreds of thousands of subscribers. And I don't pay attention to my own little jewel. So now I'm going to, now my entire focus of my life is to get, multiply the number of idiots in this room of this type by about 10,000. And I need some help doing it. And I would welcome all to do it. Now, if you're interested, all you have to do is talk to me or Terry or talk to you right now. Don't talk to me about anything. You know, the only reason I'm doing this is because there's so many amateurs there. I mean, we get into this, you can order the tapes right from Susan, have them go on mag tape and everything. I don't want to grow the business. I don't want nothing. I want to be able, I want to be able to write my letter and go out and take pictures of girls for my tiki tacky little calendar. Isn't that a useful calendar at that little month of March in there? I want to do that. And then I want to do things of the nature that I am interested in. Now, does anybody have to leave right away?
